top.thesis: Arbutus Biopharma Corp (ABUS) screens constructively, with 9 independent categories pointing favourably — led by financial health, competitive position, catalysts. The weighted composite score is 74/100, and 9 of the scored categories agree on the bullish side versus 0 on the bearish side — conviction here rests on that breadth of agreement, not on any single category. This is a research/screening read of independent evidence, not a prediction or investment advice.

top.fundamental_summary: Category scores — growth 72/100, business quality 49/100, financial health 100/100. Most recent period (Q1 2026): revenue $179.1M, net income $169.7M, free cash flow $-8.1M, operating margin 94.4%.
top.technical_summary: Category scores — price technicals 70/100 (confidence 100%), momentum 82/100 (confidence 80%). Latest close 4.87 on 2026-07-02; the available 501-bar window ranged 2.71–5.10. The last close sits at the 90% point of that range (0% = window low, 100% = window high).
top.confidence_explanation: Of 11 scored non-risk categories, 9 agree on the bullish side (9 bullish, 0 bearish); signal-agreement is 69% and overall conviction confidence is 65%. Conviction is intentionally driven by how many INDEPENDENT categories agree, not by any single extreme score. Mean data coverage across categories is 79% — the fraction of required inputs that were actually present; missing data lowers, never inflates, the score. Confidence would RISE if: filling the thin-coverage categories (ownership / insider activity); raising confidence in ownership / insider activity; more independent categories moving into agreement. Confidence would FALL if: categories began to disagree, fresh data warnings appeared, or already-strong categories weakened toward neutral.
monitoring_checklist: Next earnings report: confirm revenue and earnings do not deteriorate (vs Q1 2026: revenue $179.1M, net income $169.7M).
Operating-margin trend: watch whether it holds above the latest 94.4%; sustained compression would weaken the quality and value reads.
Insider activity: latest disclosed transaction was a award by Nguyen Tuan on 2026-02-02; watch for further open-market buys (supportive) or cluster selling (a warning).
Breakout level: a sustained close above 5.10 (the 501-bar window high) would confirm upside technicals; last close was 4.87.
Breakdown level: a sustained close below 2.71 (the 501-bar window low) would invalidate the technical read and warrant reassessment.
Catalyst follow-through: track resolution of 'Catalyst: regulatory approval: 0.27' and whether it plays out as the evidence suggests.

top.thesis: VCI Global Ltd (VCIG) screens constructively, with 7 independent categories pointing favourably — led by historical analogs, financial health, competitive position. The weighted composite score is 72/100, and 7 of the scored categories agree on the bullish side versus 3 on the bearish side — conviction here rests on that breadth of agreement, not on any single category. The main offsetting concern is momentum (score 11/100). This is a research/screening read of independent evidence, not a prediction or investment advice.

top.confidence_explanation: Of 11 scored non-risk categories, 7 agree on the bullish side (7 bullish, 3 bearish); signal-agreement is 21% and overall conviction confidence is 20%. Conviction is intentionally driven by how many INDEPENDENT categories agree, not by any single extreme score. Mean data coverage across categories is 68% — the fraction of required inputs that were actually present; missing data lowers, never inflates, the score. Confidence would RISE if: filling the thin-coverage categories (historical analogs, business quality); more independent categories moving into agreement. Confidence would FALL if: categories began to disagree, fresh data warnings appeared, or already-strong categories weakened toward neutral.

Operating-margin trend: begin tracking once margin data is reported (not currently available).
Insider activity: latest disclosed transaction was a buy by HOO VOON HIM on 2026-05-26; watch for further open-market buys (supportive) or cluster selling (a warning).
Breakout level: a sustained close above 1,058,400.00 (the 501-bar window high) would confirm upside technicals; last close was 2.32.
Breakdown level: a sustained close below 0.71 (the 501-bar window low) would invalidate the technical read and warrant reassessment.
Catalyst follow-through: track resolution of 'Catalyst: insider buying: 0.13' and whether it plays out as the evidence suggests.

top.thesis: Protalix BioTherapeutics, Inc. (PLX) screens constructively, with 8 independent categories pointing favourably — led by financial health, ownership / insider activity, momentum. The weighted composite score is 69/100, and 8 of the scored categories agree on the bullish side versus 0 on the bearish side — conviction here rests on that breadth of agreement, not on any single category. This is a research/screening read of independent evidence, not a prediction or investment advice.

top.fundamental_summary: Category scores — growth 42/100, business quality 63/100, financial health 99/100. Most recent period (Q1 2026): revenue $33.8M, net income $18.3M, free cash flow $21.3M, operating margin 62.7%, gross margin 87.8%.
top.technical_summary: Category scores — price technicals 51/100 (confidence 100%), momentum 87/100 (confidence 80%). Latest close 2.32 on 2026-07-02; the available 501-bar window ranged 0.82–3.19. The last close sits at the 63% point of that range (0% = window low, 100% = window high).
top.confidence_explanation: Of 11 scored non-risk categories, 8 agree on the bullish side (8 bullish, 0 bearish); signal-agreement is 65% and overall conviction confidence is 63%. Conviction is intentionally driven by how many INDEPENDENT categories agree, not by any single extreme score. Mean data coverage across categories is 91% — the fraction of required inputs that were actually present; missing data lowers, never inflates, the score. Confidence would RISE if: more independent categories moving into agreement. Confidence would FALL if: categories began to disagree, fresh data warnings appeared, or already-strong categories weakened toward neutral.
monitoring_checklist: Next earnings report: confirm revenue and earnings do not deteriorate (vs Q1 2026: revenue $33.8M, net income $18.3M).
Operating-margin trend: watch whether it holds above the latest 62.7%; sustained compression would weaken the quality and value reads.
Insider activity: latest disclosed transaction was a buy by Bashan Dror on 2025-12-19; watch for further open-market buys (supportive) or cluster selling (a warning).
Breakout level: a sustained close above 3.19 (the 501-bar window high) would confirm upside technicals; last close was 2.32.
Breakdown level: a sustained close below 0.82 (the 501-bar window low) would invalidate the technical read and warrant reassessment.
Catalyst follow-through: track resolution of 'Catalyst: insider buying: 0.04' and whether it plays out as the evidence suggests.

top.thesis: MainStreet Bancshares, Inc. (MNSB) screens constructively, with 8 independent categories pointing favourably — led by historical analogs, financial health, competitive position. The weighted composite score is 68/100, and 8 of the scored categories agree on the bullish side versus 1 on the bearish side — conviction here rests on that breadth of agreement, not on any single category. The main offsetting concern is management (score 39/100). This is a research/screening read of independent evidence, not a prediction or investment advice.

top.fundamental_summary: Category scores — growth 94/100, business quality 44/100, financial health 99/100. Most recent period (FY2026): revenue $838.7M, net income $45.4M, free cash flow $34.3M, operating margin 7.3%, gross margin 27.3%.
top.technical_summary: Category scores — price technicals 52/100 (confidence 100%), momentum 57/100 (confidence 80%). Latest close 19.91 on 2026-07-02; the available 501-bar window ranged 10.24–28.27. The last close sits at the 54% point of that range (0% = window low, 100% = window high).
top.confidence_explanation: Of 11 scored non-risk categories, 4 agree on the bullish side (4 bullish, 1 bearish); signal-agreement is 38% and overall conviction confidence is 24%. Conviction is intentionally driven by how many INDEPENDENT categories agree, not by any single extreme score. Mean data coverage across categories is 88% — the fraction of required inputs that were actually present; missing data lowers, never inflates, the score. Confidence would RISE if: more independent categories moving into agreement. Confidence would FALL if: categories began to disagree, fresh data warnings appeared, or already-strong categories weakened toward neutral.
monitoring_checklist: Next earnings report: confirm revenue and earnings do not deteriorate (vs FY2026: revenue $838.7M, net income $45.4M).
Operating-margin trend: watch whether it holds above the latest 7.3%; sustained compression would weaken the quality and value reads.
Insider activity: latest disclosed transaction was a exercise by Anderson Sheila Mae on 2026-07-01; watch for further open-market buys (supportive) or cluster selling (a warning).
Breakout level: a sustained close above 28.27 (the 501-bar window high) would confirm upside technicals; last close was 19.91.
Breakdown level: a sustained close below 10.24 (the 501-bar window low) would invalidate the technical read and warrant reassessment.
Catalyst follow-through: track resolution of 'Catalyst: earnings beat: 0.31' and whether it plays out as the evidence suggests.
